clinical_coherence.notes: 42M left-handed African American with temporal lobe seizures. The clinical presentation is consistent with left TLE (auditory aura, behavioral arrest, right-sided automatisms). However, there are demographic-medical inconsistencies: the patient is listed as 42-year-old male but has PCOS (polycystic ovary syndrome) and is on combined oral contraceptives - conditions/medications exclusive to females. This is a clear template error. The prolonged febrile seizure history at age 2 is an appropriate MTS risk factor.

tool_output_consistency.notes: EEG, MRI, video-EEG, PET, and neuropsych are all concordant for left temporal lobe epilepsy with hippocampal sclerosis. Auditory aura localizes to lateral temporal neocortex. Drug interaction check for carbamazepine is appropriate and correctly notes enzyme induction effects.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G40.109 correct. Differential is well-calibrated. Critical actions and reasoning points are clinically accurate. Carbamazepine as the initial AED choice with discussion of enzyme induction effects is educationally relevant.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Classic left TLE with MTS and concordant multimodal findings. Straightforward difficulty is appropriate.

demographic_plausibility.notes: CRITICAL: 42-year-old MALE patient has PCOS and is on combined oral contraceptives and metformin for PCOS with insulin resistance. This is a sex-medication/diagnosis mismatch from template error.

educational_value.notes: Good teaching case for auditory aura as lateral temporal localization and carbamazepine considerations, but the demographic error undermines case credibility.

issues_found: CRITICAL: Male patient (42M) has PCOS diagnosis and is on combined oral contraceptives and metformin for PCOS - sex-condition/medication mismatch
Left-handedness is appropriate to note but its implications for surgical planning could be more explicitly addressed
strengths: Auditory aura as lateral temporal lobe localization is a distinct teaching point
Carbamazepine drug interaction considerations are educationally valuable
